types.name: Security Vulnerability
types.description: An exploitable weakness that lets an attacker breach confidentiality, integrity, or availability (e.g. injection, broken auth, secret exposure).

types.name: Reliability Bug
types.description: A state or correctness defect that makes the system produce wrong results, crash, or behave non-deterministically under normal use.

types.name: Performance Risk
types.description: A latency, resource, or scaling concern (e.g. N+1 queries, unbounded growth, hot-path inefficiency) that degrades behavior as load or data grows.

types.name: Maintainability
types.description: A code-health or changeability concern (e.g. duplication, dead code, tangled coupling) that raises the cost of future changes without being a runtime defect.

types.name: Test Gap
types.description: Missing or insufficient test coverage for behavior that should be verified, leaving regressions undetected.

types.name: External Dependency
types.description: A risk originating in a third-party dependency (e.g. a CVE in a pinned package); pairs with the 'needs scanner validation' classification and should be confirmed by a dependency scanner rather than treated as proven.
